Package io.forja.components.dateAndTime.fxTimePicker

Class FxTimePicker

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.layout.Pane
javafx.scene.layout.VBox
io.forja.components.dateAndTime.fxTimePicker.FxTimePicker
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class FxTimePicker extends javafx.scene.layout.VBox
A styled time input with hour and minute spinners, optional seconds, and an optional 12-hour AM/PM toggle. Layout mirrors FxTextField — a framed row on top, helper/error label beneath.

FxTimePicker is a composite VBox of two rows:

  1. A framed HBox carrying the leading icon (if any), the hour Spinner, ":" separator, minute Spinner, optional ":" + second Spinner, and optional AM/PM toggle Label.
  2. A FxLabel carrying the helper or error text — auto-hidden when both are empty.

The value is an ObjectProperty holding a LocalTime or null for empty. Spinner edits update value in real time. Hour range is 0..23 for 24-hour mode or 1..12 for 12-hour mode. Minutes wrap by getStepMinutes() (default 1).

setMin(java.time.LocalTime) / setMax(java.time.LocalTime) clamp the returned value on set (the spinners themselves stay full-range; clamp is applied on assignment). When seconds are hidden (isShowSeconds() == false), the seconds field is fixed to 0.

Setting setErrorText(java.lang.String) to a non-empty value auto-flips the variant to InputVariant.ERROR; clearing does not auto-revert.

Builder usage:

     
      FxTimePicker t = FxTimePicker.builder()
          .value(LocalTime.of(9, 30))
          .use24Hour(false)
          .stepMinutes(5)
          .leadingIcon("fth-clock")
          .helperText("Meeting time.")
          .build();
See Also:
  • InputVariant
  • FxTimePicker.Builder

    • getValue

      public LocalTime getValue()
      Returns the current time, or null.

    • setValue

      public void setValue(LocalTime v)
      Sets the value; null clears. Clamped to [min, max].
